_comment: S9 Hashed-callsign cross-cycle resolution — rr-linked-cycle-effectiveness-scenario. A Type 4 (i3=4) CQ announcement of a fictional Q-prefix nonstandard callsign is played in cycle N; a standard Type 1 message referencing that callsign's 22-bit ihashcall hash is played in cycle N + gap_cycles. This is design D1's PATH 2 (confirmatory resolution check) — deliberately thin per design.md: the cross-cycle table mechanism is already proven deterministic by f-001-hashed-callsign-resolution's own unit tests, so this scenario's only job is confirming 'resolved' fires correctly over a real live-audio channel, at one or two comfortably-decodable SNR points. It is NOT a decode-rate characterisation (see S11 for that — D1 path 1).
name: Hashed-callsign cross-cycle resolution
response: resolved / not-resolved (placeholder) / announcement-not-decoded, conditional on announce-cycle decode (see analyse.py _analyse_hashed_callsign_resolution)
description: 2 pairs x 5 trials. Confirmatory check only (design D1 path 2): does a genuinely-decoded Type 4 announcement's nonstandard callsign resolve correctly when referenced by hash in a later cycle, over the real VB-CABLE live-audio channel (not hand-packed PCM fed directly to DecodeAll, as f-001's own tests do). gap_cycles=1 for both pairs per design.md's Open Questions recommendation (start with one gap value; add a second only if the first raises a question).
